title: INJURED WILDLIFE REPORTING - 211info
text: OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND WILDLIFE INJURED WILDLIFE REPORTING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Takes reports of sick, injured, stranded, or orphaned land and marine animals. Removing or capturing wildlife from the wild and keeping it in captivity without a permit is against the law. It is considered a Class A misdemeanor, punishable by up to one year in jail and a $6250 fine. 4034 Fairview Industrial Drive SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (866) 968-2600 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (866) 968-2600 Wildlife Health Hotline Toll-free (800) 720-6339 Reporting sick or injured wildlife Other (800) 452-7888 Oregon State Police Reporting Line Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: -Call or visit website for more information and a list of local licensed wildlife rehabilitators -Call before picking up or moving any wildlife -Call the Oregon State Police reporting line for additional help or to report a violation FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Animal Control Sick Animal Care Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER REGULATION - FISHING AND HUNTING LICENSES DOMESTIC ANIMAL SERVICES - REPORT WILDLIFE SIGHTINGS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND WILDLIFE
